calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing Re Petition For Relief
Off Calendar Mar-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOSE DEL CID Notice Of Hearing Re Petition For Relief.

Petitioner's petition for relief from the government claims requirement under Government Code section 946.6 is denied. (See Dept. of Water & Power v. Superior Court (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1288, 1291 ["Relief from failure to timely present a government tort claim is available only if the applicant establishes by a preponderance of the evidence the failure was 'through mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect.'"].)

First, no competent evidence supports the petition. Plaintiff's "affidavit" is not under penalty of perjury in compliance with CCP 2015.5. Second, plaintiff fails to demonstrate mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Plaintiff asserts that it took him time to find a mechanic. But the record shows he obtained a mechanic's estimate on December 27, 2023, well within the 6-month time period. (Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice ("RJN") C.) Plaintiff claims that there was a language barrier, but his "affidavit" does not mention this issue, is in clear English, and there is no evidence that it was originally provided in another language or translated before execution. In any event, defendant provides instructions to claimants in Spanish. (Defendant's RJN, A, B.) Lastly, plaintiff's claimed lack of knowledge of how to obtain legal support is unexplained and unconvincing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Continued To May-14-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-12-2024 Continued To Mar-12-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-06-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant City and County of San Franciso's demurrer to Plaintiff Jose Maria Del Cid's "Initial Complaint" is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff's "Initial Complaint" is not a proper pleading for the relief that plaintiff seeks. Plaintiff has leave to file a petition for relief (not a complaint) in compliance with Government Code section 946.6 supported by competent evidence (e.g., not inadmissible hearsay). (Dept. of Water & Power v. Superior Court (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1288, 1291 ["Relief from failure to timely present a government tort claim is available only if the applicant establishes by a preponderance of the evidence the failure was 'through mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect.'"].)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After hearing, an order is signed.
